digest.component_or_area: release pipeline build-once artifact identity + Hatchling provenance hook
propositions: The release artifact and the CI-tested code come from different build paths, so the published wheel/sdist is never installed or functionally tested before publish.
The wheel built from the extracted sdist (which has no .git) bakes COMMIT = None, so the published wheel loses its source commit provenance.
The provenance hook writes _build_info.py unconditionally with no preservation of an existing baked commit and no failure when the commit is absent.
The single existing test builds WheelBuilder directly against the checkout, bypassing the failing sdist-to-wheel path, so the None-commit defect is uncaught.
The fix requires build-once: build wheel+sdist once, test the exact artifacts in a clean env, and publish the same bytes verified by hash before publishing.
The hook needs a four-step commit precedence (env var, preserve-existing, git, None) with fail-fast only when REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT is set but empty/unresolvable.
